reasoning_model_1: The model accurately reproduces the content of the script without any errors. Regarding the core paralinguistic feature (pitch adjustment), the audio begins with a relatively higher, enthusiastic pitch for the phrase '妈妈，快看' (0:00-0:02). Immediately following this, there is a distinct and sudden drop in pitch at the 0:02 mark as the speaker transitions to '我画了一个大大的太阳...'. The voice becomes significantly deeper and more resonant, effectively simulating an 'extremely low pitch' for the remainder of the clip. This dynamic adjustment is sharp, noticeable, and aligns perfectly with the instruction to change pitch suddenly.
reasoning_model_2: The model fails significantly in content accuracy. It hallucinates the instructional prompts as part of the spoken text, saying '高音妈妈快看' (High pitch Mom look) and '突然转为极低音' (Suddenly turn to extremely low pitch) within the speech itself. This is a critical error where the model failed to separate the stage directions from the script. Furthermore, regarding the core feature, while the voice starts high (a high-pitched female timbre), the drop after the hallucinated instruction is not 'extremely low'; it merely returns to a standard speaking volume/pitch, failing to achieve the dramatic contrast requested.
model_comparison: Model 1 followed all instructions perfectly. It delivered the correct text and successfully demonstrated the dynamic adjustment of paralinguistic features by shifting from an enthusiastic tone to a very deep, low-pitch voice exactly where requested. Model 2 failed completely by reading the prompt instructions aloud ('高音...', '突然转为极低音...') instead of acting them out, and its pitch control was weak compared to Model 1. Model 1 is the clear winner.
